microsoft_ft: Scaled Azure's recovery validation program into a **platformized internal system**, standardizing workflows across data-heavy, interdependent services to sustain 45+ annual drills and drive **$14M+ business impact** across Databricks, Walmart, SAP, and NetApp.

Led **0→1 delivery** of an internal Resilience Automation Platform—defining product requirements, self-service scheduling specs, and adoption criteria—reducing operational toil by 30% and transitioning execution to a scalable, maintainable model.

Pioneered Azure's first proactive resilience testing capability, delivering a rack-level drill program in **4 months at 94% recovery rate**, surfacing critical hardware defects and establishing a new standard for continuous validation at scale.

Directed **14 cross-org recovery executions** under executive visibility, including serving as bridge lead for a sovereign-cloud network isolation test tied to a $1.5B+ enterprise contract with complex multi-team technical dependencies.

Built an internal **AI agent for drill planning**, restructuring planning workflows to cut cycle time by 39% and increase drill capacity by 21%—demonstrating forward-looking automation applied to a high-interdependency internal platform.

Defined and tracked success metrics—latency, reliability, and stakeholder satisfaction—across internal platform initiatives, communicating roadmap progress and tradeoffs to cross-functional stakeholders at all levels of the organization.
microsoft_2023: Championed adoption of **AI-driven code generation** workflows across 14 Azure service teams, conducting user demos and training sessions that drove measurable utilization and saved **37 engineering hours** monthly on internal tooling.

Shaped the product roadmap to include **intent-based YAML generation** by facilitating 11+ structured user interviews with Azure engineering teams, translating ambiguous internal needs into clear, actionable feature specifications.

Improved **data quality and retrieval reliability** by enforcing rigorous metadata standards and migrating internal documentation to an AI-powered semantic search platform, cutting lookup time by 83% across engineering teams.
microsoft_2022: Generated **$3M in accelerated revenue** and launched regions 28% faster by securing cross-functional alignment on a unified automation prioritization framework, coordinating execution across 140+ teams with competing internal demands.

Built a product strategy to reduce operational toil during region launches—conducting discovery with 20+ service teams, identifying **81 hours of manual effort** per region, and roadmapping automation for the highest-impact critical paths.

Engineered a **Power BI dashboard** to surface operational toil data across 140+ teams, enabling leadership to identify automation gaps, prioritize high-value initiatives, and track execution progress against internal improvement targets.
amazon_robotics: Delivered **zero operational downtime** across a 2,000+ unit pilot by owning the legacy OS migration strategy—mapping technical dependencies across 1,200+ stations and sequencing delivery to protect reliability of a highly interdependent system.

Facilitated Agile ceremonies including sprint planning and retrospectives, maintaining a prioritized backlog that resolved **40+ high-priority tickets** and stabilized the developer intake process across engineering and operations teams.

Drove alignment across IT, Operations, and Engineering to implement **automated CI/CD pipelines**, accelerating the software deployment cycle by 25% and improving release reliability for a large-scale distributed robotics platform.

Translated ambiguous cross-team requirements into structured delivery plans with clear milestones and dependency maps, ensuring predictable execution across a technically complex, multi-stakeholder internal infrastructure program.
pro_painters: Increased job bookings by **26%** by redesigning the end-to-end scoping and invoicing lifecycle for 200+ monthly proposals, implementing a structured CRM process that improved data completeness and operational reliability.

Reduced **Customer Acquisition Cost by 13%** and boosted conversions by 2.7% by executing a digital-first go-to-market strategy—optimizing the website and Google profile to improve funnel quality and measurable downstream outcomes.

Improved leadership pricing models by conducting **financial profitability analysis** across 1,000+ project records, identifying margin leaks and surfacing data-driven recommendations that informed future operational and pricing decisions.
